bins.name: ANOPR jurisdictional concerns
bins.desc: The Michigan AG supports NASUCA's view that the ANOPR raises jurisdictional concerns about the scope of DOE's authority and its potential impact on existing FERC frameworks.
quotes: The Attorney General replies to express support for NASUCA's comments as to jurisdictional concerns presented by the ANOPR's arguments and potential impact.

bins.name: Against expedited rulemaking
bins.desc: The Michigan AG endorses NASUCA's position that expedited action on the ANOPR is inappropriate, implying the proceeding warrants more deliberate consideration.
quotes: The Attorney General further replies to express support for NASUCA's comments as to why expedited action would be inappropriate here.

bins.name: Ratepayer protection priority
bins.desc: The Michigan AG joins a multi-state coalition urging FERC to make ratepayer protection a central priority throughout this rulemaking, not an afterthought.
quotes: The Attorney General also replies to express support for the request raised in comment by a group of several states' attorneys general and ratepayer advocates for FERC to "meaningfully prioritize ratepayer protections during this rulemaking process."

bins.name: Cost protection for ratepayers
bins.desc: By endorsing the call to prioritize ratepayer protections, the Michigan AG signals concern that cost allocation rules for large load interconnection must guard against shifting costs to existing customers.
quotes: The Attorney General also replies to express support for the request raised in comment by a group of several states' attorneys general and ratepayer advocates for FERC to "meaningfully prioritize ratepayer protections during this rulemaking process."
